memoryArtifacts.headline: The river Yare at Langley with Buckenham Watermill and Beauchamp Arms. Reproduction of a charcoal-on-paper drawing by Susan Laughlin.
The remains of Langley Abbey, Norfolk. Date: 13 October 2011. Photo by JohnArmaghof Wikiwand.
The barn of Langley Abbey. Photo from Wikiwand article on the abbey.
Engraving on behalf of Langley Abbey, Norfolk by E. Roberts from a drawing by JS Cotman. From excursions through Norfolk in 1818.
Remains of the abbey Langley, a Premonstratensian abbey, was founded in 1195 and dissolved in 1536. Photo taken 6 June 2009 by Evelyn Simak.
Langley Abbey. The restored Cellarium, inside are lights to highlight the room. Photo taken 23 April 2011 by Ashley Dace. Wikimedia Commons.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Bateman於1277年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾艾尤爾梅時，她的父親William Bateman18歲，母親Margery Le Cementarii13歲。她與Roger Kerdeston結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1347年在英國英格蘭諾福爾可易亞斯特 盧斯頓享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可明日香。

nameMeanings.name: Bateman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bateman, Bat(te)man, and in the West Midlands of England Batemon, a pet form of Bate or Batte with the hypocoristic suffix -man.

variants.name: Batman
meanings: Turkish: from batman, the name of an old measure of weight, regionally also meaning ‘big jug’ and ‘heavy’, applied as an ornamental name or perhaps as a nickname for a heavy man.

variants.name: Bate
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Bate. It could represent an unrecorded Old English name Bata, or a form of Batte, a short form of Anglo-Norman French names beginning with Bart-, such as Bartelmeu (see Bartholomew ) or Bartram (see Bertram ).
perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English antecedent of the modern English dialect bate ‘river island or land liable to flooding’.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Bateman was born in 1277, in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Bateman, was 18 and her mother, Margery Le Cementarii, was 13. She married Roger Kerdeston. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1347, in East Ruston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Langley, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Bateman est née en 1277, à Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Bateman, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margery Le Cementarii, avait 13 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Kerdeston. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1347, à East Ruston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Langley, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat Roger Kerdeston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1347 im Alter von 70 Jahren in East Ruston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Langley, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Batemanが1277年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアエウエルメで生まれた時，父William Batemanは18歳，母Margery Le Cementariiは13歳であった。Roger Kerdestonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1347年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクエアスト ルストンで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクラングリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Bateman(이)가 1277년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Bateman(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Le Cementarii(은)는 13세였다. 그녀는 Roger Kerdeston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1347년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 이스트 러스턴에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 랭글리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Bateman родилась в 1277 г. в местности ювелм, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Bateman было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Le Cementarii, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Kerdeston. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1347 г. в местности эаст рустон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности лангли, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
